req.title: Open checking account
req.statement: A verified customer shall be able to open a checking account with an initial deposit between $25 and $50,000; deposits outside this range shall be rejected.
preconditions: customer.verified=true

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines two orthogonal axes of variation: (1) deposit amount validity [<25, 25, 25-50000, 50000, >50000] and (2) customer verification state [verified, unverified]. I apply EP to partition deposit amounts into valid/invalid ranges, BVA at the exact boundaries $25 and $50,000, NEG for the unverified-customer 403 path, DT to cover the combined condition space (verified+valid, verified+invalid-low, verified+invalid-high, unverified+valid), and STC to confirm the post-creation state transition (account moves from non-existent to ACTIVE with notification dispatched). Each test is idempotent via a unique customer fixture that is torn down after the test.

tests.name: test_open_checking_account_valid_deposit_mid_range
preconditions: customer.verified = true
deposit amount $1000 is within the valid equivalence class [25, 50000]
actions: Create a verified customer via <fixture: verified_customer>
expected: Response body contains account_id and status=ACTIVE
welcome-notification dispatched (notification service mock receives one event)
tests.executable:
def test_open_checking_account_valid_deposit_mid_range(verified_customer, api_client, notification_mock):
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 1000, "currency": "USD"}
    resp = api_client.post("/accounts/checking", json=payload, headers=verified_customer["auth_headers"])
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "ACTIVE"
    assert notification_mock.received_event("welcome", body["account_id"])

tests.name: test_open_checking_account_deposit_below_minimum_rejected
preconditions: customer.verified = true
deposit amount $10 is below the minimum valid deposit of $25
actions: Create a verified customer via <fixture: verified_customer>
expected: Response body contains error code DEPOSIT_BELOW_MINIMUM
trace: R-FIN-001: deposit < 25 must be rejected with 422
tests.executable:
def test_open_checking_account_deposit_below_minimum_rejected(verified_customer, api_client, notification_mock):
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 10, "currency": "USD"}
    resp = api_client.post("/accounts/checking", json=payload, headers=verified_customer["auth_headers"])
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "DEPOSIT_BELOW_MINIMUM"
    assert notification_mock.event_count() == 0

deposit amount $60000 is above the maximum valid deposit of $50000
actions: Create a verified customer via <fixture: verified_customer>
expected: Response body contains error code DEPOSIT_ABOVE_MAXIMUM
trace: R-FIN-001: deposit > 50000 must be rejected with 422
tests.executable:
def test_open_checking_account_deposit_above_maximum_rejected(verified_customer, api_client, notification_mock):
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 60000, "currency": "USD"}

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "DEPOSIT_ABOVE_MAXIMUM"
    assert notification_mock.event_count() == 0

deposit amount $25 is exactly the lower boundary of the valid range
actions: Create a verified customer via <fixture: verified_customer>
expected: Response body contains account_id and status=ACTIVE
trace: R-FIN-001: BVA lower bound ON point

def test_open_checking_account_at_minimum_boundary_accepted(verified_customer, api_client, notification_mock):
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 25, "currency": "USD"}
    resp = api_client.post("/accounts/checking", json=payload, headers=verified_customer["auth_headers"])

    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "ACTIVE"
    assert notification_mock.received_event("welcome", body["account_id"])

tests.name: test_open_checking_account_one_below_minimum_boundary_rejected
preconditions: customer.verified = true
deposit amount $24 is one unit below the lower boundary
actions: Create a verified customer via <fixture: verified_customer>
expected: Response body contains error code DEPOSIT_BELOW_MINIMUM
trace: R-FIN-001: BVA lower bound OFF point (24 < 25)

tests.name: test_open_checking_account_one_above_maximum_boundary_rejected
preconditions: customer.verified = true
deposit amount $50001 is one unit above the upper boundary
actions: Create a verified customer via <fixture: verified_customer>
expected: Response body contains error code DEPOSIT_ABOVE_MAXIMUM
trace: R-FIN-001: BVA upper bound OFF point (50001 > 50000)

tests.name: test_open_checking_account_unverified_customer_forbidden
preconditions: customer.verified = false
deposit amount $1000 is within the valid equivalence class [25, 50000]
actions: Create an unverified customer via <fixture: unverified_customer>
expected: Response body contains error code CUSTOMER_NOT_VERIFIED
trace: R-FIN-001: precondition customer.verified=true; when false expect 403
R-FIN-001: error pathway customer not verified
tests.executable:
def test_open_checking_account_unverified_customer_forbidden(unverified_customer, api_client, notification_mock):
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 1000, "currency": "USD"}
    resp = api_client.post("/accounts/checking", json=payload, headers=unverified_customer["auth_headers"])
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "CUSTOMER_NOT_VERIFIED"
    assert notification_mock.event_count() == 0

tests.name: test_open_checking_account_state_transition_to_active
preconditions: customer.verified = true
customer has no existing checking account (pre-state: ACCOUNT_NON_EXISTENT)
deposit amount $500 is within the valid range
actions: Create a verified customer with no accounts via <fixture: verified_customer_no_accounts>
trace: R-FIN-001: account created outcome maps to ACTIVE state
STC: covers state edge ACCOUNT_NON_EXISTENT -> ACTIVE
tests.executable:
def test_open_checking_account_state_transition_to_active(verified_customer_no_accounts, api_client, notification_mock):
    create_payload = {"initial_deposit": 500, "currency": "USD"}
    create_resp = api_client.post("/accounts/checking", json=create_payload, headers=verified_customer_no_accounts["auth_headers"])
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    account_id = create_resp.json()["account_id"]
    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}", headers=verified_customer_no_accounts["auth_headers"])
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "ACTIVE"
    assert notification_mock.event_count_for("welcome", account_id) == 1
